hypothesis: A static attention scorecard can decide whether attention should be the next bounded kernel without board access. With hidden size 64 and window64, v1k and v4k attention is smaller than the existing MLP lane, while the full TinyStories output head remains the larger streamed-output problem.

commands.cmd: sed reads of AUTONIGHT_STATUS.md and required h2 evidence artifacts

commands.failure: bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted
bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted
bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted

assumptions.dsp_cycle_model: one int8 MAC per DSP lane per cycle; softmax excluded from lower-bound MAC cycles

validation.method: manual arithmetic plus sed readback; Python, git, rg, redirection writes, and apply_patch remain blocked by bwrap or write failures
validation.hardware_block_reason: no board access in this run

decision.recommendation: Do not start a full attention stack yet. When executable tooling recovers, first prototype a single-layer window64 score and value attention core using precomputed int8 Q, K, V tensors and an int8 KV-cache image, streaming one head at a time and keeping only per-head score/probability scratch. Add QKV and output projections after the KV-cache and softmax boundary is measured.
decision.rationale: The dense QKV and attention output projections are close to already validated HxH matrix-vector work. The uncovered risk is the KV-cache access pattern plus softmax/value streaming. Full TinyStories attention is significant but remains much smaller than the full vocab output projection.
decision.next_gate: Recover Python and generate this same scorecard from scripts/task6/score_attention_residual_scaling.py, then run a Verilator-only window64 attention-core testbench if the executable scorecard agrees.
